title: BACKND Base SDK initialization
summary: Describes the explicit SDK initialization API surface, including synchronous and asynchronous initialization, optional custom settings, and basic success and failure patterns.
capabilities: differentiate sync and async initialization options
outline the role of BackendCustomSetting fields
warn when exact field names should be rechecked upstream
prerequisites: BACKND Base SDK is already installed
Credentials or equivalent custom settings are available before the first SDK call
The app initializes the SDK only once during runtime
common_pitfalls: Mixing inspector-based settings with BackendCustomSetting assumptions
Treating initialization as optional before authenticated or data APIs are called
Hardcoding exact settings without checking the current official docs when freshness matters
public_notes: Exact initialization signatures should be revalidated from the official docs before code generation in a live project.
This topic is designed for routing and reasoning, not as a replacement for API reference pages.
